content.text:
## Task: Fix [high] Documentation: Stale 'temporary' comment documents a FilesController test exclusion that should already have been r
## Step: review

### TestProject.Tests/TestProject.Tests.csproj
```csproj
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net10.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
    <IsTestProject>true</IsTestProject>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.8.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" Version="2.6.6" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.5.6" />
    <PackageReference Include="coverlet.collector" Version="6.0.0" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\TestProject.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>

```
You are a fix agent. Resolve the following final-review finding.

Review dimension: Documentation
Severity: high
File: TestProject.Tests/TestProject.Tests.csproj
Title: Stale 'temporary' comment documents a FilesController test exclusion that should already have been removed

Finding:
TestProject.Tests/TestProject.Tests.csproj contains an XML comment block above an `<ItemGroup>` that excludes `Controllers\FilesControllerTests.cs` and `Controllers\FilesControllerContractTests.cs` from compilation. The comment states the exclusion is 'Temporary', explains it exists because FilesController 'is implemented by a separate task step and is not guaranteed to exist', and explicitly instructs: 'Once Controllers/FilesController.cs is present in the main project, remove this ItemGroup to re-enable these tests.' That precondition is now satisfied — `Controllers/FilesController.cs` exists and is fully implemented (all 7 endpoints), and the main `TestProject.csproj` compiles everything except `TestProject.Tests/**`. So the comment is stale and self-contradictory: its own documented removal condition has been met, yet the exclusion remains, silently preventing both FilesController test files from compiling or running. This misleads maintainers about the build state and leaves the controller's documented contract unverified.

Fix instructions (follow exactly; make targeted, minimal changes):
In file TestProject.Tests/TestProject.Tests.csproj, delete the entire XML comment block (the `<!-- ... -->` that begins with 'Temporarily exclude the FilesController test files from compilation.') AND the immediately following `<ItemGroup>` that contains the two lines `<Compile Remove="Controllers\FilesControllerTests.cs" />` and `<Compile Remove="Controllers\FilesControllerContractTests.cs" />`. Do not touch the other `<ItemGroup>` (the one with the `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`/`xunit` PackageReferences or the `<ProjectReference Include="..\TestProject.csproj" />`). The documented precondition ('Once Controllers/FilesController.cs is present in the main project') is now satisfied because Controllers/FilesController.cs exists in the main project, so both test files should be re-included in compilation. After editing, run `dotnet build TestProject.Tests/TestProject.Tests.csproj` to confirm the test project compiles with FilesController now available from the referenced TestProject.csproj.

## Response Format (required)
Respond with ONLY a single valid JSON object matching the schema below.
- No prose, explanations, or commentary before or after the JSON.
- No markdown code fences (```), no headings — output the raw JSON object.
- Do NOT call any tools and do NOT end your turn on a thinking block. Emit the JSON object directly as text.

JSON schema:
{ approved: Whether the implementation is accepted, feedback: Detailed review feedback, issues: Array<{ file: File with the issue, description: What needs to be fixed, severity: "critical" | "minor" /* How important the fix is */ }> }

(3) The other two `<ItemGroup>` elements (PackageReferences for Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk/xunit/etc. and the `<ProjectReference Include=\"..\\TestProject.csproj\" />`) remain untouched. (5) Both test files (FilesControllerTests.cs and FilesControllerContractTests.cs) exist under TestProject.Tests/Controllers/. (6) `dotnet build TestProject.Tests/TestProject.Tests.csproj` succeeds with 0 warnings and 0 errors, confirming the test files now compile against the FilesController exposed by the referenced TestProject.csproj.
